title: Artificial Intelligence
description: A comprehensive, university-grade course on the foundations of Artificial Intelligence, from search algorithms and knowledge representation to modern NLP and large language models. This course is placement-focused, with extensive interview preparation materials for roles like AI Engineer, ML Engineer, and Research Scientist.

modules.title: Getting Started
modules.description: Introduction and overview of Artificial Intelligence

pages.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
Artificial Intelligence — Wiki Index

modules.title: Core Lectures
modules.description: All lectures and concept pages for this course

pages.title: Heuristic Function Design: Admissibility, Consistency, and Dominance
Bidirectional Search and Meet-in-the-Middle Strategies
Local Search: Hill Climbing, Simulated Annealing, and Tabu Search
Monte Carlo Tree Search: UCT and Game Tree Exploration
Alpha-Beta Pruning Enhancements: MTD(f) and Iterative Deepening
Arc Consistency (AC-3) and Constraint Propagation Algorithms
Temporal Logic: LTL and CTL for Reasoning About Time
Forward and Backward Chaining: Rule-Based Inference
Description Logics and OWL Ontologies
Knowledge Graphs: Construction, Embedding, and Querying (SPARQL)
Markov Chains and Stationary Distributions
Hidden Markov Models: Forward-Backward and Viterbi Algorithms
Kalman Filters and Extended Kalman Filters for State Estimation
Particle Filters: Sequential Monte Carlo Methods
Conditional Independence and d-Separation in Bayesian Networks
Causal Reasoning: Interventions and Counterfactuals (do-calculus)
Q-Learning to Deep Q-Networks: Value-Based Reinforcement Learning
Policy Gradient Methods: REINFORCE and Actor-Critic Architectures
Speech Understanding: ASR Pipelines and Acoustic Modeling
Semantic Parsing: Mapping Language to Logical Forms
Image Recognition and Scene Understanding Pipelines
Classical Planning: PDDL, STRIPS, and GraphPlan
Hierarchical Task Networks (HTN) and Partial-Order Planning
Swarm Intelligence: Ant Colony Optimization and Particle Swarms
Evolutionary Strategies and CMA-ES for Black-Box Optimization
Neural-Symbolic Integration: Combining Learning and Reasoning
Federated Learning: Privacy-Preserving Distributed AI
Explainable AI (XAI): LIME, SHAP, and Interpretability Methods
AI Safety: Alignment, Robustness, and Adversarial Attacks
AI Hardware: GPUs, TPUs, and Neuromorphic Computing
Adversarial Search
AI Ethics, Safety, Fairness, and Explainability
AI in Robotics: SLAM, Motion Planning, Perception
Bayesian Networks
Computer Vision in AI: Object Detection, Segmentation, YOLO
Constraint Satisfaction Problems
Expert Systems and Rule-Based Reasoning
First-Order Logic
Evolutionary Computing: Genetic Algorithms and Swarm Intelligence
Informed Search
Intelligent Agents
Knowledge Representation
LLM-Based Agents: Tool Use, ReAct, Chain-of-Thought Prompting
Markov Decision Processes
Multi-Agent Systems: Game Theory, Mechanism Design
Natural Language Processing
Automated Planning: STRIPS, PDDL, Hierarchical Task Networks
Propositional Logic
Reinforcement Learning: Q-Learning, SARSA, Deep RL
Uninformed Search
Transformer Architecture
Reasoning Under Uncertainty: HMMs, Particle Filters, Kalman
